g.text: ESP32-P4 / DM542T Stepper Driver / NEMA 23 / 24-36 V DC motor supply (36 V optimal)
Touch I2C nets: GPIO7 = SDA, GPIO8 = SCL
GT911 @ 0x5D + ADS @ 0x48 | ext I2C = P4 GPIO7/8 nets (see NOTES 9-10)
36 V optimal / 24 V works / min 5 A
V = 2.0-5.5 V supply | G = GND | S/D = I2C clock / data
S + D: many boards include 10k pull-ups
I2C 0x48 when ADDR is on PCB to GND (typical)
-> V G S D + pedal pot to pad 0 (AIN0)
1. DM542T input wiring follows the terminal labels:
PUL- / DIR- / ENA- = common GND bus
2. ENA is active LOW (LOW = motor enabled).
3. All GNDs must share a common bus.
5. Motor current set via SW5-SW8 on driver.
6. E-STOP opens NC contact on trigger -> HW
disables ENA within 0.5 ms via ISR.
7. ADS1115 castellated: V G S D / pads 0-3.
Pedal pot wiper goes to pad 0 = AIN0.
8. I2C 0x48 is typical. If not, tie ADDR to GND.
9. GT911 OK but no ADS ACK: check D -> GPIO7, S -> GPIO8.
10. Still no ACK: swap ADS S and D once at CN3.
11. JP1: ADS D/S to pins 23 + 25 only, not 24/26 (C6).
Firmware uses GPIO7/8 I2C, not a second bus.
